data.title: Radian Group Earnings Call: Core Strength, Bold Deal
data.description:
Radian Group Inc ((RDN)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Radian Group’s latest earnings call struck a notably upbeat tone, underscoring strong core performance in mortgage insurance, robust returns on equity and healthy book value growth. Management balanced this optimism with candid discussion of rising defaults and a sharp, but manageable, drop in holding company liquidity following its transformative Inigo acquisition.

Mortgage Insurance In‑Force Hits Record Levels

Radian’s mortgage insurance in‑force climbed to an all‑time high of $283 billion, up 3% from a year ago, as the company benefited from steady demand. New insurance written reached $55 billion in 2025, a 6% increase versus 2024, with Q4 NIW of $15.9 billion outpacing the prior‑year quarter’s $13.2 billion.

Strong Earnings and EPS Boosted by Buybacks

Net income from continuing operations reached $159 million in Q4, or $1.15 per share, and $618 million, or $4.39 per share, for the full year, reflecting resilient profitability. Earnings per share were further supported by an 8% year‑over‑year decline in share count, highlighting the accretive impact of active repurchases.

Solid Returns on Equity and Book Value Expansion

Return on equity remained attractive at 13.5% in Q4 and 13.1% for the full year, underscoring efficient capital deployment. Book value per share rose 13% year‑over‑year to $35.29, and management noted that dividends added roughly another 3% of value, reinforcing total shareholder return.

Revenue Growth, Premium Strength and Investment Income

Total revenues reached $301 million in Q4 and $1.2 billion for the year, supported by the highest level of net premiums earned in more than three years at $237 million in the quarter. The company’s $6.1 billion investment portfolio generated $249 million of net investment income in 2025, providing a meaningful earnings tailwind in a higher‑rate environment.

Credit Trends and Favorable Reserve Development

Credit performance stayed constructive, with Radian maintaining a 7.5% default‑to‑claim assumption on new defaults and recording a $57 million provision for Q4 new defaults. This was partially offset by $35 million of favorable reserve development, resulting in a net provision expense of $22 million as strong cure trends supported a 92.5% cumulative cure assumption.

Capital Returns and Share Repurchase Activity

Radian Guaranty returned $795 million to the holding company in 2025, enabling significant capital deployment to shareholders. Radian Group distributed about $576 million via dividends and buybacks, including the repurchase of roughly 13.5 million shares for $430 million, signaling confidence in intrinsic value.

Inigo Acquisition Positions Radian for Transformative Growth

The company closed its acquisition of specialty insurer Inigo for about $1.67 billion, funded entirely with existing liquidity rather than new equity. With Inigo’s estimated tangible equity at $1.16 billion, management paid roughly 1.4 times tangible book and expects the deal to double annual revenue, be accretive to EPS and deliver mid‑teens returns through the cycle.

Robust Capital Buffer and Conservative Leverage

Radian ended the year with a $1.6 billion PMIERs capital cushion, comfortably above regulatory requirements and reinforced by an excess‑of‑loss reinsurance deal on about $373 million of older policies. The leverage ratio declined to 18.3%, and management plans to keep it below 20% by year‑end 2026, preserving balance sheet flexibility.

Portfolio Simplification and Liquidity Rebuild Plan

The company is progressing with plans to divest its Mortgage Conduit, Title and Real Estate Services businesses by Q3 2026, streamlining its focus. Before closing Inigo, holding company liquidity stood at $1.8 billion, and management expects at least $600 million of dividends from Radian Guaranty in 2026 to restore liquidity and repay a $200 million credit facility draw.

Rising Defaults and Higher Default Inventory

Radian reported about 14,200 new defaults in Q4, pushing total defaults to roughly 25,000 loans and raising the portfolio default rate to 2.56%. Management framed the increase as largely driven by normal seasonality and seasoning of the larger in‑force book, but investors will watch closely to see whether this trend moderates.

Provision Expense Persists Despite Positive Reserve Releases

Even with $35 million of favorable reserve development in Q4, the company posted a net provision expense of $22 million due to higher provisioning on new defaults. This dynamic underscores how an expanding default inventory can still pressure earnings, even when prior‑period books exhibit strong cures and reserve releases.

Liquidity Tightness After Inigo Close

Following the Inigo purchase, holding company liquidity dropped sharply from $1.8 billion at year‑end to about $350 million, increasing near‑term dependence on subsidiary dividends. Management emphasized that expected cash flows from Radian Guaranty should comfortably rebuild liquidity, but the interim squeeze is a key risk factor for investors to monitor.

Inigo Integration and Accounting Uncertainties

Management acknowledged that purchase accounting for Inigo is not fully finalized, with the split between goodwill and intangible assets still being worked through. This means amortization expenses and some reported results will remain somewhat uncertain in the near term, though the integration itself is expected to be relatively light.

Limited Near‑Term Visibility Into Inigo Metrics

The company refrained from offering detailed forward guidance for Inigo, pointing only to its historical mid‑to‑high‑80s combined ratios as a directional benchmark. Investors will need to wait until the first combined reporting period for clearer insight into segment performance, earnings volatility and capital needs.

Persistency and Seasonality in Portfolio Performance

Quarterly persistency eased slightly to 82% amid higher refinance activity, but management still expects overall persistency to remain strong given current rate levels. They cautioned that both persistency and cure patterns could shift as newer vintages season, suggesting that credit and revenue trends may become more sensitive to macro conditions.

Guidance and Outlook Emphasize Accretion and Discipline

Looking ahead, Radian expects Inigo to roughly double group revenues and drive mid‑teens returns, with management estimating about $170 million of pretax incremental income and meaningful ROE accretion. The company aims to maintain a $1.6 billion PMIERs cushion, keep premium yields around 38 basis points, preserve high persistency, hold leverage below 20% and potentially resume opportunistic buybacks as liquidity recovers.

Radian’s earnings call painted a picture of a mortgage insurer in strong financial shape, now layering on a bold specialty insurance platform through Inigo. While rising defaults, tighter holding company liquidity and short‑term accounting uncertainties warrant attention, the overall message was one of confidence in continued earnings power, disciplined capital management and enhanced long‑term growth prospects.

data.title: Megaport Corrects FX Assumption in H1 FY26 Results Guidance
data.description:

Megaport ( (AU:MP1) ) has issued an announcement.

Megaport Limited, a specialist in programmable, software-defined connectivity services for enterprise infrastructure, has built a global platform that links businesses, data centres, and cloud providers across over 1,000 locations. Its offering enables customers to deploy secure, scalable network and compute resources closer to users and data, targeting organisations that need flexible, on-demand connectivity.

The company issued a correction to its H1 FY26 half-year results release, clarifying that its financial guidance assumes a foreign exchange outlook of $0.70 AUD:USD for the second half of FY26. The amendment signals the FX rate underpinning Megaport’s forecasts, a key assumption for investors assessing the company’s guidance and potential currency impacts on reported results.

Oldfields Holdings Ltd ( (AU:OLH) ) has shared an update.

Oldfields Holdings Limited has requested an immediate trading halt of its securities on the ASX as it prepares an announcement related to a proposed capital raise. The halt will remain in place until either the capital-raising announcement is released or normal trading resumes on Tuesday, 24 February 2026, ensuring the market receives material funding news in an orderly and informed manner.

The move signals that Oldfields is pursuing new capital, which may impact its balance sheet, shareholder dilution, and funding capacity for future initiatives once details are disclosed. data.title: Hansoh Wins EU Approval for Key Lung Cancer Drug Aumolertinib
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited ( (HK:3692) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group announced that its innovative lung cancer drug Aumolertinib Mesylate Tablets, marketed as Ameile in China and Aumseqa outside China, has received European Union approval as a monotherapy. The authorization covers first-line treatment of adults with advanced EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer, as well as patients with advanced EGFR T790M mutation-positive disease, following a positive opinion from the EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use.

The EU approval significantly extends the global reach of Aumolertinib, which already holds multiple indications in China and marketing authorization in the U.K. This milestone strengthens Hansoh’s position in the competitive global oncology market and underscores its transition from a domestic innovator to an international player in targeted cancer therapies, with potential benefits for its growth prospects and international partnerships.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3692) stock is a Buy with a HK$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited stock, see the HK:3692 Stock Forecast page.

More about Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited

Hansoh Pharmaceutical Group Company Limited is a China-based pharmaceutical company focused on innovative therapies, particularly in oncology. Its portfolio includes Aumolertinib Mesylate Tablets, a third-generation EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor used to treat various forms and stages of non-small cell lung cancer, reflecting a strategic emphasis on targeted cancer treatments in both domestic and international markets.

Average Trading Volume: 8,280,646

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$223.4B

data.title: Rural Funds Group lifts earnings and advances development while holding FY26 guidance
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Rural Funds Group ( (AU:RFF) ) is now available.

Rural Funds Group reported a 6.8% rise in net property income to $48.6m for the half-year to 31 December 2025, driven mainly by additional rental income from macadamia developments. Earnings jumped to $44.1m, largely due to interest rate swap revaluations and asset sale gains, while adjusted funds from operations and distributions were in line with full-year forecasts and adjusted net asset value edged up to $3.10 per unit.

The group continued to recycle capital, with $60.7m of asset divestments at or above book value helping to fund $70.7m of capex and slightly reduce pro forma gearing to 39.1%. Progress on major developments, including the first-stage completion of the Kaiuroo project and ongoing macadamia orchard expansion at Rookwood Farms, along with a largely leased, long-WALE portfolio, underpins its position in defensive food production property and supports reaffirmed FY26 distribution guidance.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RFF) stock is a Hold with a A$2.11 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rural Funds Group stock, see the AU:RFF Stock Forecast page.

More about Rural Funds Group

Rural Funds Group is an Australian agricultural real estate investment trust listed on the ASX that owns a diversified portfolio of farming properties across five agricultural sectors and multiple climatic zones. Its strategy is to generate income and capital growth by developing and leasing agricultural assets under predominantly long-term, triple-net leases, with a target of 4% annual distribution growth and quarterly distribution payments.

Average Trading Volume: 633,867

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$787.2M

data.title: VSee Health Invests in GoMyRx Digital Prescription Platform
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

VSee Health ( (VSEE) ) has issued an announcement.

On December 26, 2025, VSee Health, Inc. and its subsidiary iDoc Virtual Telehealth Solutions, Inc. entered into a Managed Services Agreement with GoMyRx, Inc., a digital prescription fulfillment platform within the GoMyDocs healthcare ecosystem. Under this agreement, VSee will provide platform administration, customer and user support, third‑party vendor coordination, and reporting and governance services to GoMyRx, billing monthly for actual expenses plus a 10% margin.

The Managed Services Agreement runs through December 26, 2027, with an option for either party to extend it month‑to‑month for up to six additional months, while GoMyRx retains the right to terminate the deal at any time with 20 days’ notice. On January 16, 2026, VSee further deepened its collaboration with GoMyRx by agreeing to purchase $2.0 million of GoMyRx common stock from Go Biz Holdings, LLC in a private transaction, securing a 10% ownership stake and aligning VSee’s strategic and financial interests with the growth of the digital prescription platform.

The most recent analyst rating on (VSEE) stock is a Sell with a $0.33 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on VSee Health stock, see the VSEE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VSEE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VSEE is a Underperform.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance (losses, negative operating cash flow, and negative equity) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation offers limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend, while recent corporate actions provide only a small offset.

To see Spark’s full report on VSEE stock, click here.

More about VSee Health

VSee Health, Inc. operates in the telehealth industry through subsidiaries such as iDoc Virtual Telehealth Solutions, Inc., providing digital health and virtual care technology and services. The company focuses on supporting healthcare platforms with capabilities like platform administration, customer and user support, and coordination with third-party vendors across digital health ecosystems.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from Cemex SAB ( (CX) ).

On February 18, 2026, Cemex repurchased 5,050,000 Ordinary Participation Certificates (CPOs) on the Mexican market at a weighted-average price of MXN$21.8158 per CPO, for a total of MXN$110.17 million, or about U.S.$6.41 million at the prevailing exchange rate. The transaction, executed through Casa de Bolsa BBVA México under a shareholder-approved buyback program from March 25, 2025, underscores Cemex’s ongoing capital management efforts and may signal confidence in its valuation while committing to disclose any future repurchases in line with Mexican regulations.

The company’s decision to continue operating under the existing share buyback framework reflects an emphasis on shareholder returns and balance-sheet optimization. By actively repurchasing CPOs and pledging timely disclosure of subsequent operations, Cemex maintains transparency with investors and reinforces its positioning as a disciplined issuer in the Mexican capital market.

The most recent analyst rating on (CX) stock is a Buy with a $14.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cemex SAB stock, see the CX Stock Forecast page.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

BSP Financial Group Ltd. ( (AU:BFL) ) has provided an announcement.

BSP Financial Group Ltd. has released its 2025 Annual Report, highlighting its strategic focus on driving prosperity across Papua New Guinea and the wider South Pacific through inclusive and modern banking services. The report underlines BSP’s market-leading positions in PNG and the Solomon Islands, its growing presence in Vanuatu, and its commitment to community leadership and digital innovation as key pillars of its ongoing regional expansion and service delivery.

The Group details extensive physical and digital infrastructure, including broad branch, ATM and agent networks that underpin access to banking in often underserved markets. By emphasising both financial performance and social responsibility, BSP positions itself as a central player in the region’s economic development, reinforcing its role in improving financial inclusion while sustaining its leadership in key Pacific banking markets.

More about BSP Financial Group Ltd.

BSP Financial Group Ltd. is a leading financial institution in Papua New Guinea and the South Pacific, providing contemporary banking services to retail, business, corporate and institutional customers. Founded in 1916 and headquartered in PNG, it is the largest company on the PNG Stock Exchange, operating 85 branches, more than 300 local agents and 339 ATMs, alongside extensive digital banking offerings across PNG, the Solomon Islands and Vanuatu.
